folio.label: 59r
folio.l: They han hire sette / and bidden hire lerne saile

note.notetxt: 342The colour of ink in f59r/v is lighter than the one on 62r.ODR

folio.l: Out of Surrie , ageynward to Itaile
¢ A certeyn tresore , that she thidder ladde
And sothe to seyn , vitaille gret plentee
They han hire ©euen , & clothes eke she hadde
And forth she seiletú , in the salt See
O my Custaunce , ful of benignytee
O Em¹oures , ©onge dougúter deere
He æ is lord ouer fortune / be thy steere
¢ She blissed hire , and witú ful pitous vois
Vn to the Croos of cést / thus seide she
O cleer , o welful auter , holy croys
Reed of the lambes blood , ful of pitee
That wesshe the world , fro æe olde iniquite
Me fro the fende , and fro his clawes kepe
That day æat I shal drenchen / in the depe
¢ Victorious tree , ¸teccione of trewe
That oonly worthy were / for to bere
The kyngö of heuene , with hise woundes newe
The whit lamb æ hurt was / witú a spere
Flemer of feendes , out of hi~ and heere
On whiche thy lymes , feithfully extenden
Me kepe , and ©eue me mygút / my lif to amenden
¢ ñeres and dayes , fleetith this creature
Thurgú out the see / of Grece , vn to the strayte
Of Marrok / as it was hire auenture
O many sory meel , now may she bayte
Aftir hire detú , ful often may she wayte
Er that the wylde wawes , wyln hire dryue
Vn to the place , there she shal arryue
¢ Men mygút asken , why she was nou©t slayne
Eke atte feest , whoo mygút hire body saue
And I answere , to that demaunde a gayne
Who saued Danyel , in the horrible caue
There euäy wygút saw he , maistä and knaue
Was witú the leou~ / freet , er he a sterte
No wygút but god , that he bar in his herte
¢ God list to shewe , his wonderful myracle
In hire , for we shulde / seen his mygúty werkes
Crist whicú æat is , to euäy harm triacle
By certeyn menes oft , as knowen clerkes
Dotú thyngö for certeyn ende , that ful derk is
To ma~nes wyt / that for oure ignoraunce
Ne konne nougút knowe , his prudent purueaunce
¢ Now sithe she was nougút , at the feest I slawe
